explanation:
Personal finance and investment use universal mathematical principles (compound interest, time value of money, risk-return optimization). This calculator applies standard industry formulas used by professional bond traders and portfolio managers.

## How It's Calculated

Modified duration measures the sensitivity of a bond's price to changes in yield. It tells you what percentage change in price you'll see for a 1% move in interest rates.

This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking for reasonable ranges and alerting you to unusual values.

## Typical Benchmarks by Time Horizon

| Horizon | S&P 500 | 10-Year Bonds | Cash |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4–5% | 3–5% |
| 5 years | ~8% avg | ~4% | Near inflation |
| 10 years | ~9–10% | ~4% | Below inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Core Investment Principles

- **Stick to your plan** during market stress. Emotion is the worst advisor.
- **Diversify**: don't concentrate in one asset, sector, or country.
- **Keep costs low**: expense ratios over 1% annually consume 30% of your returns over 30 years.
- **Think long-term**: stocks work over 10+ years, not 1 year.
- **Rebalance once yearly**, not more frequently.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide. For critical financial decisions, consult a professional advisor. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.a: Modified duration measures how much a bond's price will change when interest rates move by 1%. It's expressed as a percentage change in price.

faq.q: How does modified duration affect bond prices?
faq.a: Higher modified duration means greater price volatility. If rates rise, bonds with higher duration lose more value. If rates fall, they gain more.

faq.q: Why is modified duration important for investors?
faq.a: It helps you understand your interest rate risk. Use it to compare bonds and match your risk tolerance with your portfolio holdings.

faq.q: What's the difference between duration and modified duration?
faq.a: Duration is the weighted average time to receive cash flows. Modified duration adjusts this mathematically to show actual price sensitivity to rate changes.

faq.q: How do I use modified duration for portfolio management?
faq.a: Use it to immunize portfolios against rate risk, construct bond ladders, or match your bond duration to your investment horizon.

faq.q: What happens to modified duration when rates fall?
faq.a: Modified duration itself doesn't change when rates fall, but it determines how much your bond's price will rise if rates decline further.

faq.q: Does modified duration work for all bond types?
faq.a: It works best for straight bonds with fixed payments. Bonds with embedded options (like callable bonds) require more complex analysis.

faq.q: How does coupon frequency affect modified duration?
faq.a: More frequent coupon payments reduce duration because you receive cash flows sooner, resulting in less price volatility.

faq.q: What's the relationship between duration and bond volatility?
faq.a: They're directly related. A bond with 7-year duration is roughly 7 times more volatile than a 1-year bond when interest rates move.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
